title: Daily Market Digest: May 23, 2026
subtitle: Market closes higher on peace talks and strong earnings; Fed gets new Chair

items.headline: Wall Street rallies for an eighth consecutive weekly advance, driven by optimism over Middle East peace talks and robust corporate earnings.
items.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average achieved a record closing high, while the S&P 500 continued its upward trend. Technology and AI-related stocks remained strong. However, consumer sentiment has reportedly fallen to a record low amidst persistent inflation concerns and elevated oil prices. Kevin Warsh has been sworn in as the new Federal Reserve Chair. This information is for journalistic purposes only and not financial advice.

items.headline: Dell Technologies
items.summary: Shares surged ahead of anticipated robust earnings, fueled by confidence in its position in the AI boom.

items.headline: HP Inc.
items.summary: Rallied amidst strong performance from US computer makers.

items.headline: Estée Lauder
items.summary: Jumped after announcing the end of merger discussions with Spanish perfume maker Puig.

items.headline: Ross Stores
items.summary: Reported strong first-quarter profit and revenue, exceeding expectations due to robust customer traffic.

items.headline: Nvidia
items.summary: The semiconductor leader experienced a slight dip in its stock price.

items.headline: Kevin Warsh Sworn In as New Federal Reserve Chair
items.summary: Kevin Warsh officially took office as the Chair of the US Federal Reserve on Friday, May 23, 2026. His tenure begins at a critical juncture for the American economy, marked by surging petrol prices and high inflation. Fed Governor Christopher Waller indicated that the central bank might consider a rate hike, suggesting a shift away from an 'easing bias' in policy outlook.

items.headline: Healthcare, Utilities, Industrials, and Technology Lead Market Gains; Communication Services and Consumer Staples Lag
items.summary: Among the eleven major S&P sectors, healthcare, utilities, industrials, and technology stocks were the primary drivers of gains. Notably, technology and semiconductor shares continued to bolster Wall Street's performance. Conversely, communication services and consumer staples sectors finished lower.

items.headline: US Consumer Sentiment Falls to Record Low Amidst Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Despite an advancing stock market, a University of Michigan survey revealed that U.S. consumer sentiment plummeted to a record low. Households expressed increasing worry about persistent inflation, exacerbated by expensive oil prices. This widening divergence between Wall Street and Main Street reflects ongoing economic anxieties.

items.headline: Brent Crude Stabilizes Above $100 as Middle East Peace Talks Progress
items.summary: Brent crude oil for August delivery saw a 0.7% increase, settling at $100.21. Global energy markets are closely watching developments in US-Iran peace talks, which have shown signs of progress, contributing to a volatile but currently upward trend in oil prices.
